title: Drenched Chewy Canadian Pasta
description: A Mediterranean-inspired Canadian dish featuring herbes-de-provence and a pasta-water-emulsion, perfect for a rainy day.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pappardelle pasta according to the package instructions until al dente.
Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining the pappardelle.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat and saute the garlic until fragrant.
Add the herbes-de-provence to the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the canned diced tomatoes, vegetable broth, and reserved pasta water, and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the sauce simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in the grated mozzarella cheese until melted and well combined.
Add the cooked pappardelle pasta to the skillet, tossing to coat with the sauce.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
